insight: Morocco's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup has been notable, as they managed to gain momentum in both of their breaks, with a mean increase of 0.250 points per minute. This suggests that the team is able to effectively use the breaks to regroup and refocus, leading to improved performance on the field. The fact that they were not pressing before either of their breaks, but still gained momentum afterwards, indicates that the breaks themselves are having a positive impact on their gameplay, allowing them to come back stronger and more cohesive. Overall, the data implies that Morocco is able to capitalize on hydration breaks to shift the momentum of the match in their favor, which could be a key strategic advantage for the team.
